frameworks.name: CISA Zero Trust Maturity Model
frameworks.description: The CISA ZTMM provides a gradient of implementation across five pillars to assist agencies in developing ZT strategies and plans.

frameworks.name: NIST SP 800-207
frameworks.description: NIST SP 800-207 defines Zero Trust Architecture and provides a roadmap for federal agencies and organizations adopting ZTA.

frameworks.name: DoD Zero Trust Reference Architecture
frameworks.description: The DoD ZT Reference Architecture defines the seven pillars of ZT for defense and national security environments.

frameworks.name: NCSC Zero Trust Principles
frameworks.description: The NCSC Zero Trust Architecture guidance outlines eight principles for designing and deploying ZTA in UK organizations.

frameworks.name: ENISA Zero Trust Guidelines
frameworks.description: ENISA guidelines on Zero Trust for EU member states and organizations, aligned with the EU cybersecurity strategy.

frameworks.name: NIS2 Directive
frameworks.description: EU NIS2 Directive establishes cybersecurity requirements for essential and important entities, with ZT-aligned access controls and incident management.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
frameworks.description: ISO 27001:2022 includes ZT-relevant controls in Annex A covering access control, cryptography, network security, and threat intelligence.

pillars.label: Identity
pillars.description: User and non-person entity authentication, authorization, and lifecycle management

pillars.label: Devices
pillars.description: Device health, compliance posture, and endpoint security

pillars.label: Networks
pillars.description: Network segmentation, traffic inspection, and access controls

pillars.label: Applications & Workloads
pillars.description: Application access, API security, and workload identity

pillars.label: Data
pillars.description: Data classification, governance, encryption, and loss prevention

pillars.label: Visibility & Analytics
pillars.description: Logging, monitoring, threat detection, and automated response
